title: "Woern's Asteroid"

'''"Woern's Asteroid"''' was one of the largest [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] in the [[Drup Asteroid Field]], a part of the [[Drup's Star system/Legends|Drup's Star system]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Saijo sector/Legends|Saijo sector]]. The [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] composition of the asteroids in the Drup Asteroid Field interfered with the [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] of [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in its vicinity.

The [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Laerron Woern]] made use of that property, establishing a [[Woern's pirate base|base]] for his [[Woern's Pirates|pirate gang]] inside the asteroid that was named after himself. Around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], a group of individuals discovered the existence of the pirate base on "Woern's Asteroid," leading to a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Assault on Woern's pirate base|assault]] on the asteroid.

==Description==
"Woern's Asteroid" was the name given to one of the largest [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] in the [[Drup Asteroid Field]]. Like the rest of the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]], it was situated in the [[Drup's Star system/Legends|Drup's Star system]],<ref name="WbC" /> a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Saijo sector/Legends|Saijo sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> "Woern's Asteroid" [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Binary star system/Legends|binary stars]] [[Drup's Star]] and [[Drup's Companion]], taking five hundred [[Standard day/Legends|standard days]] to do so, although the asteroid itself did not rotate.<ref name="WbC" />

"Woern's Asteroid" had light [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] and no [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The [[Temperature/Legends|frigid]] asteroid's terrain featured [[Cave/Legends|caves]] and natural tunnels as well as arid [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]]. The [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] composition of the objects in the Drup Asteroid Field interfered with standard [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] readings, making it difficult for [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in the area to detect other vessels.<ref name="WbC" />

==History==
{{RPGseed}}
By [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> the [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]] [[Laerron Woern]] and his [[Woern's Pirates|pirate gang]] had established a [[Woern's pirate base|base]] inside the asteroid, which was named after the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] leader. At some point, a group of individuals discovered the presence of Woern's pirate gang in the Drup Asteroid Field. The individuals eventually brought proof of the existence of the pirate base on "Woern's Asteroid" to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], which dispatched [[Warship/Legends|warships]] on an [[Assault on Woern's pirate base|assault]] on the facility.<ref name="WbC" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
The population of "Woern's Asteroid," which numbered two hundred individuals of the Elomin, [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]], [[Human/Legends|Human]], and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Species/Legends|species]], did not import or export anything to the base located inside the asteroid.<ref name="WbC" />

==Locations==
{{Main|Woern's pirate base}}
The mineral properties of the Drup Asteroid Field prevented the detection of Laerron Woern's pirate base on "Woern's Asteroid," with the starships of the pirates themselves also being affected by the sensor interference. The pirates, however, were aware of several routes through the asteroid field that allowed them to safely travel to their base. In addition, Woern himself discovered that the interference could be dampened by readjusting a starship's sensors, thus allowing one to locate the base.<ref name="WbC" />

==Behind the scenes==
"Woern's Asteroid" was introduced in the [[1993]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The book includes an adventure seed for use with ''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game''. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Drup's Star [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the asteroid, in grid square J-20.<ref name="TEA" />
